title: Gabor-Based Learnable Sparse Representation for Self-Supervised Denoising
authors: Liu, S., Cheng, S., Alkhalifah, T. A.
org: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
abstract: Proposes a Gabor-based learnable sparse representation network for self-supervised seismic denoising by constraining Gabor filter parameters

title: Self-Supervised Ground-Roll Noise Attenuation Using Self-Labeling and Paired Data Synthesis
authors: Oliveira, D. A. B., Semin, D. G., Zaytsev, S.
abstract: Proposes a two-stage self-supervised framework using U-Net for ground-roll region detection and cGAN for noise attenuation via synthetic paired data

title: Deep learning with soft attention mechanism for small-scale ground roll attenuation
authors: Yang, L., Fomel, S., Wang, S., Chen, X., Chen, Y.
abstract: Proposes a supervised deep learning framework with soft attention residual learning for small-scale ground roll attenuation

title: Unsupervised ground-roll attenuation via implicit neural representations
authors: Li, J., Liu, D., Sacchi, M. D.
abstract: Proposes an unsupervised ground-roll attenuation method using implicit neural representations with NMO correction and horizontal derivative regularization

title: A reweighted damped singular spectrum analysis method for robust seismic noise suppression
authors: Huang, W.-L., Zhou, Y.-X., Zhou, Y., Liu, W.-J., Li, J.-D.
abstract: Proposes reweighted damped singular spectrum analysis (RD-SSA) for robust seismic incoherent/erratic noise suppression, combining damping operator and Tukey’s bisquare iterative reweighting

title: Seismic multiple suppression based on a deep neural network method for
authors: Wang, K., Hu, T., Wang, S., Wei, J.
org: Peking University, China University of Petroleum (Beijing)
abstract: Proposes a deep neural network (DNN) with data augmentation training for marine seismic multiple suppression and primary reconstruction by adding noise and rotation to enhance robustness

title: An in-depth study of U-net for seismic data conditioning: Multiple removal by moveout discrimination
authors: Durall, R., Ghanim, A., Ettrich, N., Keuper, J.
abstract: Proposes a U-Net model trained on synthetic data for postmigration multiple removal via moveout discrimination, achieving strong generalization and preserving data features without parameter tuning

title: Accelerating the U-Net based adaptive subtraction with transfer learning for removing seismic multiples in field data
authors: Li, Z., Ma, J., Qi, Z., Zhang, L., Xie, F., Yang, J.
abstract: Proposes transfer learning accelerated U-Net for adaptive multiple subtraction, reusing prior parameters to reduce epochs, achieving same accuracy with ~40% less computation time for field data

title: Unsupervised dual learning for seismic data denoising in the absence of labelled data
authors: Zhao, Y. X., Li, Y., Wu, N.
abstract: Proposes unsupervised DeGANs based on CycleGAN for seismic denoising without labeled data; trained on unpaired real noisy and synthetic clean data, suppresses random noise and ground roll thoroughly with almost no false signals

title: Simultaneous Suppression of Random Noise and Ground Roll With a Fuzzy Logic-Guided Deep Network
authors: Zuo, X., Ma, J., Liao, Z., Chen, X.
abstract: Proposes DASDNet with fuzzy logic encoder, STFT-DWT time-frequency decomposition, and variance-aware attention to simultaneously suppress random noise and ground roll
